description: Just moved to Seattle. First MLS match. Knows nothing about the Sounders.

emails.subject: Your tickets for Sounders vs. Vancouver — everything you need to know
Thanks for coming! Here's your match recap

social_media.notes: No social media connection to the club yet.

content_preferences.notes: Clicked on stadium guide link — likely values practical, orientation-style content.

campaign_history.notes: Too new for campaign targeting. No historical data to segment on.

player_affinity.notes: No data yet. Player interest to be developed through first-match experience.

section_305_info.atmosphere: Family-friendly area, good sightlines, less intense than lower bowl

first_time_fan_tips.arrive_early: Gates open 90 minutes before kickoff. Arrive at least 60 minutes early for your first time.
first_time_fan_tips.match_duration: About 2 hours total — two 45-minute halves plus a 15-minute halftime. No timeouts.
first_time_fan_tips.what_to_wear: Dress in layers. Seattle evenings can be chilly even in spring. Rave green (Sounders color) if you have it, but not required.
first_time_fan_tips.mobile_tickets: Tickets are on your phone via the Sounders app or email.
first_time_fan_tips.supporter_culture: You'll hear chanting and drums — that's the Emerald City Supporters in Sections 121-123. It's fun, not intimidating. Everyone is welcome.

highlights: Jordan Morris opened the scoring in the 23rd minute with a clinical finish
Leo Chu scored a spectacular solo goal in the 58th minute — dribbled past three defenders
Yeimar headed in a corner in the 78th minute to seal it
Stefan Frei kept a clean sheet with one outstanding save in the first half
Attendance: 33,891. Great atmosphere for a Cascadia Cup match.
match_attended.experience_notes: First MLS match experience. The 3-0 win was an ideal first impression — goals, atmosphere, clean sheet.

key_moments_for_newcomer.newcomer_context: The stadium erupted. This is what it feels like when the home team scores.
The goalkeeper made a diving save. The crowd gasped, then cheered. That's Stefan Frei — he's been doing this for 12 years.
Leo Chu picked up the ball and ran past three defenders before scoring. The crowd was on their feet before he even shot.
A set piece goal from a corner kick — the tall defender rose above everyone. 3-0, the stadium was celebrating.

conversion_factors: Had a great first experience (3-0 win, exciting match)
Opened both emails — engaged and curious
Clicked stadium guide — values practical info
New to city — looking for community and things to do
Coworker connection — social proof and potential repeat attendance partner
next_steps: Portland match (Apr 12) — the biggest rivalry, best atmosphere of the season, ideal second match
Player introduction content — she saw Morris, Chu, Yeimar, and Frei play; build on those
Community angle — she's new to Seattle, the Sounders can be part of her new life here
Keep it simple — don't assume MLS knowledge, explain context
derived_insights.optimal_approach: Warm, welcoming, assume nothing about soccer knowledge. Build on the positive first experience. Make the next match feel like a natural next step, not a sales pitch.
avoid: Heavy stats or tactical analysis — she's brand new
Assumed knowledge of MLS, rules, or player history
Hard sell or urgency pressure — she needs to feel invited, not pushed
Jargon (VAR, offsides trap, false 9, etc.) without explanation
